🌿

Theo’s Adventures

🌿

Choose Your Own Adventure

Theodore Earnest O’Hare — Theo to everyone who knows him — is seventeen, restless, and always falling asleep over his homework. Each time he drifts off, he wakes up inside the book he was studying. Your choices determine how his story unfolds.

The Trial of Socrates

Theo standing trial alongside Socrates in Plato’s Apology and Crito

Play ➔

The Hemlock Cup

Theo witnessing Socrates’ final hours in Plato’s Phaedo

Play ➔

The Golden Mean

Theo debating virtue and the good life in Aristotle’s Ethics

Play ➔

The Golden World

Theo unraveling tragedy and rhetoric in Aristotle’s Poetics

Play ➔

The First Cause

Theo grappling with being and causation in Aristotle’s Metaphysics

Play ➔

The Evil Genius

Theo doubting everything alongside Descartes in the Meditations

Play ➔

The Prince’s Gambit

Theo navigating the treacherous courts of Machiavelli’s Prince

Play ➔

The Dangerous Perhaps

Theo walking the razor’s edge of Nietzsche’s philosophy

Play ➔

Fall of the Republic

Theo caught in the conspiracies of Rome’s dying Republic, from History & Humanities I

Play ➔

The Parchment and the Sword

Theo surviving the upheavals from Alfred the Great to the Reformation, from History & Humanities II

Play ➔

The Balance of Power

Theo entangled in the power struggles from Elizabeth I to the Great War, from History & Humanities III

Play ➔

The War That Changed the World

Theo lost in the devastation of the Thirty Years’ War, from History & Humanities III

Play ➔

Words at War

Theo confronting the machinery of wartime propaganda, from The Sage of Baltimore and War: A Debate in Primary Sources

Play ➔

Theo and the Ten Days

Theo trading tales with Boccaccio’s storytellers during the plague, from Tales of the Decameron

Play ➔

Immortal Lines

Theo decoding the mysteries hidden in Shakespeare’s Sonnets

Play ➔

Between the Lines

Theo stepping inside the worlds of Chekhov, Joyce, and Hemingway, from Reading the Modern Short Story

Play ➔

The Green Light

Theo chasing illusions through Fitzgerald’s glittering Long Island, from The Great Gatsby

Play ➔

The Sage’s Apprentice

Theo matching wits with H.L. Mencken in smoky 1920s Baltimore, from The Sage of Baltimore

Play ➔

The Thinker’s Labyrinth

Theo testing his mind against the full arsenal of critical thinking, from The Thinking Toolkit

Play ➔

The Sacred Roundtable

Theo convening the great religions at a mystical amphitheater, from Religious Roundtable

Play ➔

The Cloister and the Cell

Theo seeking wisdom among monks from the Desert Fathers to the present, from The Cloister and the Cell

Play ➔
